weeklyBrief.summary: Davyn's pipeline holds 119 open opportunities with an active Partners Distribution USD-equivalent value of $187,232. The partner has 6 closed-won deals (15% win rate on closed) against 35 losses. There are 8 critical-urgency deals this week — Cable Bahamas ($2,820, CFO budget approved awaiting signature), Tucker Energy ($4,000, June 29 close), UNICOMER ($10,000, June 29 close), Rhand Credit Union (vendor selection end of June), Port Authority ($3,500, June 29 close), Mother's Jamaica ($2,900, competing ODOO evaluation), and IPrint ($1,150, June 29 close). 16 additional high-priority deals in advanced stages include Bermudez Group ($18,900), Beharry Holdings (€20,000), Seprod (€30,000), and ANSA McAL ($13,410). Umar leads with 46 deals, Nicholas with 8, Sekou with 7. DATA QUALITY: sender fill rate is 38% — deals without Davyn notes (New Deals, SDR prospects, internal pipeline) account for the gap. DATA QUALITY: lastActivityDate fill rate is 65% — 56 deals have no logged activity.

weekOverWeek._note: weekOverWeek deltas require a previous export to compare. Values reflect current snapshot totals.

topActions.action: Draft and send formal SoW to John Fernandes Ltd this week to lock in the July close date.
Call Ashley directly this week to confirm the HR and IT team meeting date.
Schedule a catch-up call with Seprod's HR and IT teams this week to restart momentum.
Escalate Tucker Energy to Umar and Leon today and confirm whether the deal can be revived before June 29.
Schedule and confirm the demo session with the ANSA McAL team before end of June.

KCL Captial - (Davyn)
deals.notes:
[2026-03-12 – umar@davyntt.com]: Spoke with Omaladee and she advise for us to do a revised SoW for 35 Users vs the original 50 Users - TBD -  @Umar Mohammed   Create new Pitch Deck/SoW

[2026-03-23 – umar@davyntt.com]: @Umar Mohammed   Complete by EOD 24th March 2026

[2026-05-22 – umar@davyntt.com]: Updated pricing submitted - awaiting final review and sign off

[2026-05-25 – umar@davyntt.com]: Omaalade indicated that they are currently reviewing another solution named Lanteria from a local company named Inova Solutions She stated that once this review if complete and budgets are done she would advise on the next steps

[2026-05-25 – umar@davyntt.com]: Email Sent to Omaalade -  Hi Omaalade, It’s always a pleasure speaking with you. I completely understand the need to evaluate options to ensure you find the perfect fit. While Lanteria offers broad functionality, it can often be heavy to manage and complex for teams to adopt. Factorial is built differently. We focus on a uniquely integrated, "all-in-one" experience specifically designed for SMBs. Where others can feel like a collection of disjointed tools, Factorial creates a seamless workflow across onboarding, time-tracking, and performance—which ultimately saves your team significant administrative time and reduces overhead. I will send over a subsequent email shortly with a detailed breakdown of how Factorial’s all-in-one approach compares to Lanteria regarding ease of use, implementation speed, and long-term cost-efficiency. In the meantime, I’d like to ensure your Chief of Staff sees exactly how these differences translate into time and cost savings for your specific team. Would they be available for a brief demo  next Monday at 11:00 AM ? Looking forward to your feedback.
